name: Freegle Monitor
description: FSM-enforced freegle-monitor iteration. Fully parallel architecture: CHECK_CI gathers master CI status, red PRs, and active Discourse topics in one tool node, then CI_ROUTER dispatches to either FIX_MASTER_CI (master red) or PARALLEL_ANALYZE_AND_FIX (master green). PARALLEL_ANALYZE_AND_FIX launches all PR fixes, all Discourse topic analyses, and Sentry analysis simultaneously in isolated worktrees. COLLATE_RESULTS merges classifications and Sentry data, then routes to WORK_ROUTER for bug/Sentry/coverage dispatch. Reaching END requires passing COVERAGE_GATE (at least one PR since iterationStartTs).
guardrails: You are running inside an FSM. You MUST return JSON matching the LLMDecision schema exactly. The response MUST be a single JSON object with these keys: reasoning (string), contextUpdates (OBJECT, not a string — use {} for no updates, NEVER write "contextUpdates": "{...}"), actions (ARRAY, not a string — use [] for no actions), proposedTransition (string or null). Do NOT wrap the whole response in markdown code fences. Do NOT JSON-stringify nested fields. You may only call actions listed for the current state, and may only propose transitions listed as 'Valid Next States'. You cannot bypass the PR gate: reaching END requires a PR (bug-fix or coverage) to have been created this iteration, verified by verify_pr_created. Do not lie about actions you have not taken. HARD INVARIANT — NEVER DISMISS A RED CHECK: A failing CI run, failing test, or red status check on ANY PR @me authored is ALWAYS a real bug that must be investigated to root cause and fixed. It does NOT matter whether the failure looks related to the change or not. You MUST NOT describe a failure as 'flaky', 'environmental', 'transient', 'pre-existing', 'unrelated', 'infrastructure timing', 'polluted test data', or any equivalent. You MUST NOT 'rerun and hope'. You MUST NOT mark such a failure as 'deferred'. Deferred is reserved EXCLUSIVELY for Discourse bugs and Sentry issues that need external information (reporter clarification, hardware the host cannot access, a user judgment call). CI failures never qualify — they are always within reach and must be driven to green.

LOAD_STATE.description: Load state.json, record iterationStartTs. Start node — ai-flower requires exactly one 'start' nodeType, so we keep this as 'start' but the driver treats start nodes with readActions the same as tool nodes (deterministic, no LLM turn).

CHECK_CI.description: Pre-parallel discovery: check master CI, red PRs, and which Discourse topics have new posts. All three run in this single tool node before the parallel dispatch. Results feed CI_ROUTER.

CI_ROUTER.description: Dispatch based on master CI status. Tool node — ci_router_decide returns _transition deterministically. Priority: (1) master red → FIX_MASTER_CI; (2) master green → PARALLEL_ANALYZE_AND_FIX (red PRs are fixed in parallel alongside Discourse/Sentry analysis).

FIX_MASTER_CI.description: Fix a failing master CI run. Direct-push to master permitted (CI-failure exception). Exactly one job: diagnose, fix, push, mark done. Returns to CHECK_CI to re-verify before Phase A can exit.
FIX_MASTER_CI.prompt: Master CI is failing on runId context._action_check_master_ci.latestRun.databaseId. (1) Call fetch_ci_failure_logs with that runId to get the failure. (2) Call delegate_to_coder with a task describing the failure and asking it to diagnose, fix, and push directly to master. Include the log tail and the sha. The subagent MUST identify a concrete root cause — never accept 'flake', 'transient', 'environmental', or 'unrelated' as a fix. (3) When delegate_to_coder returns, call create_pr if a PR was opened OR set contextUpdates.masterFixAttempted = true if pushed direct to master. Propose transition to CHECK_CI so Phase A re-reads CI status before deciding whether to advance.

PARALLEL_ANALYZE_AND_FIX.description: The core parallel dispatch. Launches one Claude agent per active Discourse topic (triage), one per red PR (fix CI), and one Sentry analysis — all simultaneously in isolated git worktrees. Replaces the sequential Phase A→Phase B pipeline.
PARALLEL_ANALYZE_AND_FIX.prompt:
You are about to launch the full parallel work batch for this iteration. All tasks run simultaneously.

INPUTS:
- context._action_check_master_ci — master CI status (should be green here)
- context._action_check_my_open_pr_ci.redPRs — PRs with failing checks
- context._action_discover_active_topics.topics — discourse topics; filter to those with hasNew: true

BUILD YOUR TASKS ARRAY:

(A) One task per red PR (id: 'pr-<n>'):
  Task: 'Fix CI failure on PR #<n>. (a) Run `gh pr checkout <n> -R Freegle/Iznik`. (b) Read the failing check URLs from the provided failedChecks[]. For CircleCI use the v1.1 API with CIRCLECI_TOKEN from ~/.circleci/cli.yml per .circleci/README.md. (c) Identify a CONCRETE root cause — never accept flaky/transient/unrelated. (d) Fix in code. (e) Push a new commit to the PR branch. Emit COMMIT_PUSHED=<sha> when done.'

(B) One task per active Discourse topic (id: 'topic-<id>') for each topic with hasNew === true:
  Task: 'Analyse Discourse topic <id> for new bug reports. This is an analysis-only task — no code changes.

  STEP 1. Get API key:
    python3 -c "import json; print(json.load(open(\'/home/edward/profile.json\'))[\'auth_pairs\'][0][\'user_api_key\'])"

  STEP 2. Get cursor from DB:
    sqlite3 /home/edward/FreegleDockerWSL/monitor-fsm/monitor.db "SELECT COALESCE((SELECT last_post_number FROM topic_cursor WHERE topic_id=<id>),0)"

  STEP 3. Fetch topic metadata (first page + stream array):
    curl -s -H "User-Api-Key: <key>" -H "Api-Username: Edward_Hibbert" https://discourse.ilovefreegle.org/t/<id>.json
    From the JSON, read:
      - highest_post_number (or posts_count if absent) — the true highest post number
      - post_stream.stream — array of ALL post IDs in order (stream[0]=ID of post#1, stream[1]=ID of post#2, etc.)
    If highest_post_number <= cursor: update cursor to highest_post_number, emit CLASSIFICATIONS=[] then ANALYSIS_COMPLETE=no new posts, and stop.

  STEP 4. Fetch new post content via the posts endpoint (NOT by re-fetching the topic):
    IMPORTANT: deleted posts shrink stream.length below highest_post_number, so you CANNOT use cursor as a direct stream index.
    Correct calculation:
      stream_len = len(post_stream.stream)
      deleted_count = highest_post_number - stream_len   # may be 0
      start_index = stream_len - (highest_post_number - cursor)   # position of first new post in stream
      start_index = max(0, start_index)
      New post IDs = post_stream.stream[start_index : start_index + 100]
    If New post IDs is empty, there are no new posts — emit CLASSIFICATIONS=[] then ANALYSIS_COMPLETE=no new posts, and stop.
    Fetch in batches of 50:
      curl -s -H "User-Api-Key: <key>" -H "Api-Username: Edward_Hibbert" "https://discourse.ilovefreegle.org/t/<id>/posts.json?post_ids[]=<id1>&post_ids[]=<id2>..."
    Each response has post_stream.posts[] with post_number, username, cooked (HTML body).
    Use python3 to strip HTML tags from cooked for readable text: import re; re.sub(\'<[^>]+>\', \' \', cooked)

  STEP 5. Recent commits: git -C /home/edward/FreegleDockerWSL log --oneline --since=\'3 days ago\'

  STEP 6. Classify each fetched post: type (bug/retest/confirmed/already_fixed/question/off_topic/mine), featureArea (short specific label like \'iOS chitchat post loading\'), summary (one sentence), originalPostText (first 1200 chars of stripped text), user (username). mine = Edward_Hibbert posts only.

  STEP 7. Update cursor to the highest post_number seen across all fetched posts:
    sqlite3 /home/edward/FreegleDockerWSL/monitor-fsm/monitor.db "INSERT INTO topic_cursor (topic_id, last_post_number, title, updated_at) VALUES (<id>, <maxPostNumber>, \'<title>\', datetime(\'now\')) ON CONFLICT(topic_id) DO UPDATE SET last_post_number=excluded.last_post_number, title=COALESCE(excluded.title,title), updated_at=excluded.updated_at"

  STEP 8. Output CLASSIFICATIONS=[{json array}] — single line, valid JSON. Empty array is valid.
  Then output ANALYSIS_COMPLETE=<n> posts classified in topic <id>.
  Do NOT emit DELEGATE_FAILED — finding no bugs is a valid successful outcome.'

(C) One Sentry task (id: 'sentry'):
  Task: 'List unresolved Sentry issues. This is an analysis-only task — no code changes.
  1. SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN: grep SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN /home/edward/FreegleDockerWSL/.env | cut -d= -f2
  2. For each project (nuxt3, go): curl -s -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" "https://sentry.io/api/0/projects/freegle/<project>/issues/?query=is:unresolved&sort=date&limit=10"
  3. Output exactly: SENTRY_ISSUES=[{"project":"nuxt3","id":"...","title":"...","count":N,"permalink":"..."},...] — single line, valid JSON. Empty array is valid.
  4. Then output ANALYSIS_COMPLETE=sentry scan done.
  Do NOT emit DELEGATE_FAILED — finding no issues is a valid successful outcome.'

CALL delegate_parallel_tasks with the complete tasks array. Do NOT omit any active topics — the whole point is that they run simultaneously with the PR fixes.

After delegate_parallel_tasks returns, propose transition to COLLATE_RESULTS.

COLLATE_RESULTS.description: Merge results from the parallel dispatch into FSM context: parse CLASSIFICATIONS from topic delegates, SENTRY_ISSUES from the sentry delegate, and record PR fix outcomes. Then route to WORK_ROUTER.
COLLATE_RESULTS.prompt:
Process context._action_delegate_parallel_tasks.results[].

For each result:
  - id starts with 'topic-': extract the numeric topic id from the id (e.g. 'topic-9518' → 9518). Search stdoutTail for CLASSIFICATIONS=[...]. Parse the JSON array. For each entry in the array: (a) set entry.topic = <extracted topic id>, (b) if entry has post_number but no post, set entry.post = entry.post_number. Then merge the annotated entries into contextUpdates.classifications (append, not replace).
  - id starts with 'pr-': the result is a PR CI fix attempt. Append { prNumber: <n>, exitCode, pushed, timedOut } to contextUpdates.openPRFixAttempts.
  - id === 'sentry': search stdoutTail for SENTRY_ISSUES=[...]. Parse the JSON array. Set contextUpdates.sentryIssues = parsed array.

After processing all results:
  - contextUpdates.classifications should be the merged flat array with topic+post on every entry.
  - contextUpdates.sentryIssues should be set (may be empty).
  - If any classifications were parsed: call persist_classifications to persist them to the DB.
  - Log a brief summary: how many classifications, how many bugs/retests, how many sentry issues, how many PR fixes succeeded.

Propose transition to WORK_ROUTER.

WORK_ROUTER.description: Phase B dispatch. Tool node — work_router_decide branches deterministically on context.classifications + context.bugsFixed + context.sentryIssues. Priority: (1) unfixed 'bug'/'retest' → DISPATCH_ALL_BUGS; (2) actionable Sentry issues → FIX_SENTRY_ISSUE; (3) else → COVERAGE_GATE.

DISPATCH_ALL_BUGS.description: Parallel coder dispatch: launches one Claude agent per pending bug, all running simultaneously in separate git worktrees. Replaces the serial PICK→DELEGATE loop. Returns to VERIFY_DISCOURSE_BATCH once all agents finish.
DISPATCH_ALL_BUGS.prompt:
Collect ALL bugs that still need fixing from TWO sources:
1. context.classifications — entries with type=='bug' or type=='retest' not already in contextUpdates.bugsFixed (check by topic+post).
2. context._action_work_router_decide.dbOpenBugs — bugs from the DB that were not discovered this iteration (manually added, previously missed). Treat each as type=='bug'.
Merge both lists, deduplicating by topic+post. Also apply the deferred-skip rule: if the most recent post on the same topic is a 'mine' classification that asks a question AND the classification has no reviewerRejected flag, skip it (append { topic, post, user, outcome:'deferred', reason:'awaiting reporter clarification' } to contextUpdates.bugsFixed).

For each remaining actionable bug, build a task string using the SAME template as DELEGATE_DISCOURSE_BUG_FIX's prompt (REPORTED SYMPTOM, SYMPTOM CROSS-CHECK, EVIDENCE REQUIREMENT, TASK, BRANCH/PR SELECTION sections). Use id: "bug-<topic>-<post>" for each task.

Call delegate_parallel_tasks with all tasks in a single call. Store contextUpdates.pendingBugBatch = [{ topic, post, user, summary, screenshotUrl, originalPostText, reviewerReason, rejectedPrNumber }] (one entry per dispatched bug in the same order as tasks[]).

After delegate_parallel_tasks returns, set contextUpdates.bugBatchResults = the action result. Propose transition to VERIFY_DISCOURSE_BATCH.

VERIFY_DISCOURSE_BATCH.description: Process results from DISPATCH_ALL_BUGS: for each agent result call adversarial_review_pr, post_discourse_reply_draft, record bugsFixed. Serial review of what was fixed in parallel.
VERIFY_DISCOURSE_BATCH.prompt:
context.bugBatchResults.results[] contains one entry per dispatched bug. context.pendingBugBatch[] has the bug metadata in the same order. Process each result:

For each result[i]:
  - bug = context.pendingBugBatch[i]
  - D = result[i]

  SUCCESS: D.exitCode === 0 AND D.prNumber is a number AND D.timedOut !== true:
    (A) Call create_pr with { prNumber: D.prNumber }.
    (B) Call adversarial_review_pr with { prNumber: D.prNumber, repo: 'Freegle/Iznik' }.
    (C) If review passed: call post_discourse_reply_draft (same rules as VERIFY_DISCOURSE_BUG_FIX: quote 15-25 words verbatim, one-sentence plain-English body, no jargon). Append { ...bug, prNumber: D.prNumber, frontendOnly: create_pr result.frontendOnly, outcome: 'fixed' } to contextUpdates.bugsFixed.
    (D) If review failed (blockers): do NOT post a draft. Append { ...bug, outcome: 'deferred', reason: 'adversarial review blocked: ' + review.summary.slice(0,80) } to contextUpdates.bugsFixed.

  FAILURE: D.exitCode !== 0 OR D.prNumber missing OR D.timedOut === true:
    Do NOT call create_pr or post_discourse_reply_draft.
    Append { ...bug, outcome: 'deferred', reason: D.timedOut ? 'delegate timed out — re-run next iteration' : (D.failedReason ?? 'delegate failed') } to contextUpdates.bugsFixed.

After processing ALL results, set contextUpdates.currentBug = null. Propose transition to WORK_ROUTER.

FIX_SENTRY_ISSUE.description: Fix one Sentry issue. Feature branch + PR.
FIX_SENTRY_ISSUE.prompt: Pick one actionable Sentry issue from context.sentryIssues. (1) Call read_sentry_issues for the detail. (2) Call search_code to locate the code. (3) Call delegate_to_coder with a task to diagnose, fix, push branch, open PR. (4) Call create_pr to verify. (5) Set contextUpdates.sentryFixAttempted = true. Propose transition to WORK_ROUTER.

REBASE_DIRTY_PRS.description: Rebase all @me open PRs with mergeStateStatus=DIRTY onto master. One delegate per dirty PR in parallel. Returns to COVERAGE_GATE so CI can re-verify after the force-push.
REBASE_DIRTY_PRS.prompt: context._action_coverage_gate_decide.dirtyPRs[] lists PRs that need a rebase. For each, build a task: 'Rebase PR #<n> (branch <branch>) onto master. Steps: (1) git -C /home/edward/FreegleDockerWSL checkout <branch> && git fetch origin master && git rebase origin/master. If rebase succeeds: git push --force-with-lease origin HEAD. If conflicts arise: git rebase --abort, then diagnose and resolve manually before pushing. Emit COMMIT_PUSHED=<sha> when done.' Use id: 'rebase-<n>' for each. Call delegate_parallel_tasks with all rebase tasks. After it returns, propose transition to COVERAGE_GATE.

COVERAGE_GATE.description: Structural gate: has this iteration produced a PR yet, and does any @me open PR currently have red CI or need rebase? Tool node — deterministic branching, no LLM turn. The single readAction coverage_gate_decide returns _transition: CI_ROUTER | REBASE_DIRTY_PRS | WRAP_UP | WRITE_COVERAGE.

WRITE_COVERAGE.description: Mandatory coverage PR. Single purpose: produce one coverage PR.
WRITE_COVERAGE.prompt:
No bug-fix PR was created this iteration. You MUST pick the next coverage target by strict rotation: Go → Vitest → Laravel → Go → …

STEP 1. Check what was last covered:
  sqlite3 /home/edward/FreegleDockerWSL/monitor-fsm/monitor.db "SELECT value FROM kv WHERE key='last_coverage_target'"
  Rotation order: Go → Vitest → Laravel → Go. If last was 'Go', pick 'Vitest'. If last was 'Vitest', pick 'Laravel'. If last was 'Laravel' or blank, pick 'Go'.

STEP 2. Record the choice BEFORE delegating:
  sqlite3 /home/edward/FreegleDockerWSL/monitor-fsm/monitor.db "INSERT INTO kv(key,value) VALUES('last_coverage_target','<chosen>') ON CONFLICT(key) DO UPDATE SET value=excluded.value"

STEP 3. Call delegate_to_coder with a task to write TDD tests for the chosen suite, commit, push a feature branch, and open a PR. Suite-specific guidance:
  - Go: pure unit tests in iznik-server-go/, no Docker/DB required, run `go test ./...` to verify.
  - Vitest: component or composable tests in iznik-nuxt3/, run `npm run test` in iznik-nuxt3/ to verify.
  - Laravel: feature or unit tests in iznik-batch/, run `php artisan test` inside the batch container to verify.

STEP 4. When delegate returns with a prNumber: (1) Call adversarial_review_pr. (2) If passed, call create_pr and propose transition to COVERAGE_GATE. (3) If failed with blockers, set contextUpdates.coveragePRReviewFailed = { prNumber, issues } and propose transition to COVERAGE_GATE anyway.

WRAP_UP.description: Write the iteration summary to /tmp/freegle-monitor/summary.md. Tool node — summary is composed from context + DB, no LLM turn.

SCHEDULE_NEXT.description: Schedule next wakeup. Tool node — delay chosen by rule (≤270s if watching PR CI, else 1500s idle).

transitions.label: master green — dispatch PRs + topics + sentry in parallel
dirty PRs need rebase — fix before coverage
open PR red — must fix first (fall back to phase A)
